name: Mimir Well
description: Mimir's Well, also known as the Well of Mimir, is a sacred well in Norse mythology associated with wisdom, knowledge, and prophecy. It is located beneath one of the roots of the world tree Yggdrasil, specifically the root that extends into Jotunheim, the land of the giants. The well is guarded and presided over by Mimir, a wise being of immense knowledge, often depicted as a severed head. The waters of the well are said to grant profound insight and understanding to those who drink from them. Odin, the Allfather, famously sacrificed one of his eyes to drink from Mimir's Well, demonstrating the great value and cost associated with attaining true wisdom. This act highlights the importance of sacrifice and dedication in the pursuit of knowledge. The well's connection to Yggdrasil reinforces its central role in the cosmos and its link to the fundamental forces of the universe. Mimir's Well represents the ultimate source of wisdom, far exceeding mortal comprehension, and its existence serves as a reminder of the continuous pursuit of knowledge and understanding in Norse cosmology. The well's location in Jotunheim also suggests that wisdom is not easily obtained and may require venturing into dangerous or unfamiliar territories. Access to this wisdom comes at a price, symbolizing that true understanding demands sacrifice and commitment.
subtitle: Source of Wisdom and Knowledge

keyMyths.title: Odin's Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: Odin sacrifices one of his eyes to Mimir in exchange for a drink from the Well of Wisdom, granting him profound knowledge and insight. This act demonstrates the importance of sacrifice in the pursuit of wisdom.

keyMyths.title: Mimir's Head
keyMyths.description: After the Aesir-Vanir War, Mimir was beheaded, but Odin preserved his head and carried it with him, consulting it for advice and wisdom. This highlights Mimir's continued importance as a source of knowledge even after death.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, containing accounts of Mimir's Well and Odin's sacrifice.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, containing accounts of Mimir's Well and Odin's sacrifice.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Norse mythology, including references to Mimir and his wisdom.
